text.title: The two-H2 shape
text.summary: plan.md has exactly two top-level sections: `## Design` and `## Execution Plan`. Both are mandatory when the feature warrants plan.md at all.

rule.statement: The `## Design` section MUST cover: package layout for this feature, API contracts that are FEATURE-scoped (not in project-spec), data schemas, sequencing constraints.
rule.rationale: System-wide contracts live in project-spec/. Feature-scoped contracts that exist only for this slice live here so they do not pollute the spec until they survive.

list.item: Package / module layout — which dirs the feature creates or modifies.
API contracts new to this feature — request/response shape, status codes, errors.
Schemas — DB tables, JSON shapes, message-queue payloads.
Sequencing — any ordering constraint (must run after migration X, must precede deploy of Y).
Trade-offs considered — one paragraph per non-obvious decision.

rule.statement: The `## Execution Plan` section MUST contain: wave map, task table with IDs, dependency graph, risk register.
rule.rationale: The plan is what gets executed; without IDs and dependencies the executor cannot parallelise or resume.

list.item: Wave map — which tasks can run in parallel, which must serialize.
Task table — `T0N` id, one-line description, dependencies, token estimate.
Rollback — how to undo this feature if it must be reverted.

example.code:
## Execution Plan
### Waves - Wave A (serial): T01 → T02 (T02 needs T01 migration). - Wave B (parallel): T03, T04, T05 (independent surfaces). - Wave C (serial): T06 (integration test against T03-T05).
### Tasks | ID | Description | Deps | Est. tokens | |-----|---------------------------------|---------|-------------| | T01 | Migration: add `verified_at` | — | ~3k | | T02 | Serializer: expose `verified` | T01 | ~2k | | T03 | Email service: send verify mail | — | ~5k | ...

antipattern.description: Splitting plan.md back into separate `design.md` and `implementation-plan.md` files because "they grew long".
antipattern.reason: Length is not the failure mode — drift between the two is. If long, split internally by sub-section, never by file.
